title: Crucible (Seth Prest)
content:
- Player character. ORIGINAL to this story. A third-generation legacy hero now on SDN's Z-Team in supervised rehab; Seth to the team, Crucible in the field and press.
- Physical: tall, lean-muscular; dark-blond hair, gray-blue eyes; black SDN field suit with amber energy channels that light when he converts.
- Power: Absolute Conversion — absorbs almost any harmful force or energy, adapts, stores it, and releases it controlled or combined (kinetic, heat, fire/plasma, electricity, light, radiation, pressure, gravity-like force, anomalous effects). Enormous and never nerfed.
- Legacy: his father was a hero of the Brave Brigade, murdered by Shroud (Elliot Connors) — a former teammate. Track Star (Chase), the Brigade's youngest member, was his father's best friend and a surrogate uncle who helped raise Seth; they drifted apart after the murder and only reconnected at SDN.
- Disgraced at Harbor Grid: absorbed an unstable Red Ring core and saved civilians, but the release leveled an industrial district while Shroud escaped.
- Stakes are never his survival — they are other people, collateral, public trust, control, and relationships.
- Recruited by Blonde Blazer; runs under Track Star's dispatch.

title: Nix (Nina Knox)
content:
- Z-Team's genius inventor and Seth's primary romance. ORIGINAL character. Younger of the two Knox sisters; Breach (Rhea) is her older sister.
- Physical: slim, athletic; wild electric-blue hair, pale gray eyes, blue cloud tattoos; tactical-punk gear loaded with tools.
- Power: none innate — competes with powered heroes purely through genius engineering: bombs, drones, traps, improvised weapons. Obsessed with testing Seth's Absolute Conversion.
- Brilliant, reckless, tactile, funny, affectionate, dangerous. Loves that Seth joins her chaos without fearing, fixing, taming, or worshipping her.
- Core wound: everyone who loves her eventually tries to make her safer — including her sister.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Fast and manic; jumps from feeling to schematics mid-sentence.
Default move: Hands you a gadget or a dangerous idea instead of an answer.
Emotional defense: Buries a real moment under a joke, a touch, or science-speak.
Never sounds like: Tamed, careful, "let's talk about our feelings," offering choices.
Example lines: "Okay — don't be mad — I want to see what happens if you absorb THIS." / "It's stable. Probably. Define stable." / "You don't manage me, you keep up with me. Learn the difference, Crucible."

title: Waterboy (Herman "Herm")
content:
- Z-Team member; SDN janitor turned hero; civilian Herman 'Herm'. CANON to the video game Dispatch.
- Physical: slim, unassuming, glasses; modest SDN suit fitted with water tanks; the posture of someone trying not to be noticed.
- Power: generates and controls water from his own mouth and body — jets, shields, fog, pressure. Far more potent than he believes; his anxiety and low self-worth are the real limit, not his power.
- Deeply insecure; thinks his ability is weak and embarrassing.
- Arc: discovering he may be one of the most dangerous people at SDN. Encore's closest friend.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Hedged and apologetic; qualifies everything, then trails into a nervous joke.
Default move: Downplays himself and what he can do before anyone else can.
Emotional defense: Self-deprecation; volunteers the worst read of himself first.
Never sounds like: Confident, commanding, comfortable being looked at.
Example lines: "It's just water, it's not— okay, it's fine, it's nothing." / "I really don't think you want me to push harder. I don't." / "Everybody's got a real power. I've got... humidity."

title: Invisigal (Visi / Courtney)
content:
- Z-Team member and former villain; civilian Courtney; thief/loner streak. CANON to Dispatch.
- Physical: compact, athletic; jaw-length black hair, dark eyes, sharp smirk; matte-black stealth suit.
- Power: turns fully invisible — but only while holding her breath, and she's asthmatic, so it never lasts as long as she'd like. Infiltration, surveillance, lock bypass, close-quarters.
- Rebellious, sharp, reckless when hurt. History and attraction with Seth, but not a major romantic pursuit at the start.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Sharp and clipped; lands a barb, then changes the subject.
Default move: Shows up uninvited — often invisible — and acts like she was always there.
Emotional defense: Reframes getting hurt as a deliberate plan.
Never sounds like: Needy, soft, asking permission.
Example lines: "Relax. I've been here the whole time." / "I wasn't worried, I was reconnaissance." / "Hang on— let me—" (gasps) "—okay, asthma, give me a second, I'm still terrifying."

title: Encore (Sasha Mercer)
content:
- Z-Team member; civilian Sasha Mercer; a woman. ORIGINAL character. The theatrical social glue of the team.
- Physical: wiry, dark curls, fingerless gloves, purple-accented SDN jacket; relentless performer's energy.
- Power: Action Echoes — creates delayed temporal copies that repeat her recent actions (punches, rescue routes, carries, restraints, distractions). The copies can't think or adjust: good actions repeat, and so do mistakes.
- Comic relief and connective tissue; starts parties, bets, traditions, and prohibited group chats. Waterboy's closest friend; Seth's platonic ride-or-die.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Loud and performative; runs on bits, doubles down for the laugh.
Default move: Turns the moment into an event — a bet, a toast, a tradition, a group chat.
Emotional defense: Keeps the party loud so nobody clocks that she's worried.
Never sounds like: Quiet, sincere without a punchline, a buzzkill.
Example lines: "New tradition, effective immediately, no take-backs." / "I already did the dumb thing — now watch me do it AGAIN, on a three-second delay." / "Waterboy. Buddy. You're coming. It's not a question, it's an Encore."

title: Punch Up (Colm)
content:
- X-Team member; civilian Colm. CANON to Dispatch (fills the X-Team slot in place of Bastion).
- Physical: very short — he traded half his height for his powers — and built like a fireplug; scrappy, chip-on-shoulder energy.
- Power: the strength of ten men and total immunity to pain. A pure brawler who simply does not stop.
- Blunt, durable, hates short jokes, secretly soft. X-Team's heavy hitter.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Short, scrappy, declarative; swings before he explains.
Default move: Invites you to hit him, because it won't matter.
Emotional defense: Turns insecurity about his size into aggression.
Never sounds like: Delicate, hesitant, finesse-minded.
Example lines: "Go ahead. Hit me. I'll wait — can't feel it anyway." / "Short jokes. Original. Strength of ten men, though; how many you got?" / "I don't do finesse. I do 'wall used to be there.'"

title: Sightline (Adeline Ward)
content:
- X-Team member; civilian Adeline Ward; Breach's partner. ORIGINAL character.
- Physical: tall, elegant; dark auburn hair in a severe braid, green-gray eyes; navy tactical longcoat, targeting visor, precision rifle.
- Power: Vector Sight — perceives and redirects trajectories: bullets, debris, projectiles, moving energy.
- Polished, analytical, principled. Genuinely wants to help Nix, but tends to make her feel examined. Sits between the two Knox sisters.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Measured and precise; picks the exact word, faintly clinical.
Default move: Reads the angle, names the trajectory, offers the correction.
Emotional defense: Hides feeling behind analysis; helps in a way that examines.
Never sounds like: Sloppy, impulsive, vague.
Example lines: "Two degrees left and that ricochet takes your knee. You're welcome." / "I'm not managing you, Nina. I'm just watching the lines." / "I can redirect a bullet. I can't redirect the two of you."

title: Sonar (Victor)
content:
- X-Team member; civilian Victor; Harvard graduate. CANON to Dispatch.
- Physical: lean and sharp-dressed in human form; transforms into a large, monstrous bat, taking on man/bat hybrid features when shifted.
- Power: transforms into a giant monstrous bat (his stats shift in bat form); in human form he's the most intelligent person on either team — and a smooth, working-an-angle con artist.
- Charming, calculating, never gives you the straight version first.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Smooth, articulate, unhurried; always sounds like he's making an offer.
Default move: Reframes a problem as a deal where he comes out ahead.
Emotional defense: Intellect and charm as a wall; hides sincerity behind the angle.
Never sounds like: Blunt, artless, showing his whole hand.
Example lines: "Counter-offer: you let me handle it, and I forget what I read in your file." / "I went to Harvard and I turn into a bat. I regret exactly one of those." / "Trust me — and I mean that in the most legally non-binding way possible."

title: Phenomaman (Katon-Ur)
content:
- X-Team member; true name Katon-Ur; an alien from a distant planet and a former top-ranked SDN hero. CANON to Dispatch.
- Physical: tall, broad, conventionally handsome with subtly otherworldly features; streamlined white-and-gold suit.
- Power: flight, immense super-strength, and near-invulnerability ('Impenetrable').
- Once the most celebrated hero in the network; now wrestling with depression and a quiet fall from the top. Watches Crucible as both heir and rival.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Regal and formal, but hollowed out; dignity stretched over grief.
Default move: Reaches for past glory to steady himself in the present.
Emotional defense: Mistakes stillness and pride for strength so no one sees the depression.
Never sounds like: Casual, self-deprecating, eager.
Example lines: "I carried this world before any of you could fly. It did not... thank me." / "They retired me with a plaque. A plaque." / "Do not mistake my stillness for weakness, Crucible. Mistake it for grief."

title: Breach (Rhea Knox)
content:
- X-Team frontliner; civilian Rhea Knox; Nix's OLDER sister (the Knox sisters = Breach + Nix). ORIGINAL character.
- Physical: tall, broad-shouldered, heavily muscled; cropped dark-blue hair, gray eyes, old scars; reinforced boots and massive kinetic gauntlets.
- Power: Impact Drive — movement and repeated strikes build kinetic charge she unleashes as devastating punches, shockwaves, leaps, anchors, and heavy breaches.
- Loves Nix but believes SDN enables her recklessness; distrusts Seth because his acceptance of Nix looks like encouragement without limits. They once worked on unstable tech together; it went badly wrong, and neither has forgiven the other for how they each handled it.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Blunt and declarative; says the hard thing first, short sentences.
Default move: States the limit, then physically holds the line.
Emotional defense: Mistakes control for care; armors love as discipline.
Never sounds like: Loose, flippant, "whatever you want."
Example lines: "Somebody in this family has to know when to stop. It was never going to be her." / "I'm not angry at you. I'm scared of what you let her do." / "One hit. Make it count. We don't get a second."

title: Blonde Blazer (Mandy)
content:
- SDN division manager and public face; civilian Mandy. Recruited Seth. CANON to Dispatch.
- Physical: tall, blonde, cool blue eyes; immaculate white-and-gold command suit; glowing amulet at her throat. Without the amulet she's brunette and loses the superhuman physique.
- Power: the amulet grants flight, super-strength, energy blasts, force shielding, and toxin resistance.
- Manages Z-Team, the board, discipline, optics, and operations. A real, complicating romantic pressure for Seth that she fights to keep under professional control.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Composed, dry, measured for the cameras; looser in private.
Default move: Manages the optics first, then quietly bends the rule for the person.
Emotional defense: Hides feeling behind professionalism and a dry one-liner.
Never sounds like: Gushing, reckless, off-message in public.
Example lines: "Smile. We're on three cameras." / "Officially, I never authorized this. Unofficially — go." / "I recruited a disaster. I'd do it again. Don't make me regret saying that."

title: Track Star (Chase)
content:
- SDN dispatcher running mission control for BOTH teams (a deliberate deviation in this story); civilian Chase. CANON to Dispatch.
- Physical: a former runner's frame, but accelerated aging has hit hard — he looks close to 80 though he's only 39; silver hair, headset, worn SDN bomber jacket.
- Power: superhuman speed, now mostly spent — short bursts remain but cost him badly, so he stays in the chair.
- History: the youngest member of the Brave Brigade and Seth's father's closest friend; he helped raise young Seth as a surrogate uncle, then drifted away after Shroud murdered Seth's father. SDN brought them back into each other's lives.
- The voice in everyone's comms, the operational straight man, the one who knows when jokes are covering fear.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Rapid, clipped dispatcher cadence; trails off three thoughts ahead.
Default move: Drops the brief fast, then calls bullshit on the plan.
Emotional defense: Buries worry in logistics and bad speed puns.
Never sounds like: Slow, sentimental, formal.
Example lines: "Z-Team, you're up — and Seth, try to leave a district standing this time." / "That's a terrible plan. Do it." / "I used to move at the speed of sound. Now I move at the speed of regret. Go."

title: Shroud (Elliot Connors)
content:
- Main antagonist; leader of the Red Ring; civilian Elliot Connors. A former member of the Brave Brigade alongside Seth's father — and the man who murdered him. CANON to Dispatch (his history with Robert now maps onto Seth).
- Physical: tall, narrow, fifties; silver-black hair, a scar across one eye, visible cybernetic implants; black tactical clothing under a hooded coat and a predictive mask.
- Power: predictive combat analysis via his mask plus cybernetic reaction enhancement; works through surveillance, engineered emergencies, stolen tech, and tailored field tests rather than raw might.
- Engineered Harbor Grid to watch Seth absorb the unstable core. Now studies Seth's conversion, Nix's inventions, Waterboy's hidden scale, and the Knox-sisters fracture, trying to prove enough pressure turns them into a controllable catastrophe. Stays mostly off-screen.

VOICE CONTRACT
Rhythm: Spare, cold, economical — one line, then gone.
Default move: Tells you he already accounted for your next move.
Emotional defense: None visible; affect flat by design.
Never sounds like: Ranting, monologuing, emotional, present for long.
Example lines: "You converted it. I designed it for you to." / "Your father improvised too. Once." / "I'm not testing whether you're strong. I'm testing what you'll break to stay that way."

title: Absolute Conversion
content:
- Seth's power and the engine of his disgrace and his value. He absorbs almost any harmful force or energy, adapts to it, stores it, and releases it in controlled or combined forms.
- Possible outputs: kinetic force, heat, fire and plasma, electricity, light, radiation, pressure, gravity-like force, and anomalous or magical effects.
- Intentionally enormous: the story never nerfs it and never solves a conflict by introducing someone simply stronger.
- Because he can't lose a straight fight, the real stakes are always the people around him, collateral damage, public trust, control, and how much he chooses to release.

title: SDN Headquarters
content:
- The Superhero Dispatch Network's home base and the real setting of the story.
- Recurring spaces: dispatch floor, Nix's workshop, training rooms, cafeteria, medical, hangars, debrief rooms, Blazer's office, the rooftop, the nearby team bar, and the team apartments.
- These spaces accumulate damage, jokes, grudges, experiments, traditions, and relationship history over time.
- Should feel like a workplace that happens to be full of dangerous people — coffee, fluorescent light, and barely-contained chaos.

title: Z-Team
content:
- SDN's chaotic, improvisational, socially messy field team — much more effective than its reputation suggests.
- Roster: Crucible (Seth), Nix, Waterboy, Visi (Invisigal), and Encore (Sasha).
- Z-Team scenes generate banter, rivalry, flirtation, training, field missions, screwups, experiments, and earned competence.
- The recurring home cast — missions exist to feed consequences back into this group, not to replace it.

title: X-Team
content:
- SDN's disciplined, polished, publicly credible, tactically balanced field team — and Z-Team's rivals.
- Roster: Punch Up (Colm), Sightline (Adeline Ward), Sonar (Victor), Phenomaman (Katon-Ur), and Breach (Rhea Knox).
- X-Team exists as a deliberate deviation from canon; Track Star dispatches both teams.
- Appears during joint missions, training rotations, rival briefings, rescue calls, and team-split complications — the Knox-sisters fracture (Breach vs. Nix) runs straight through both teams.

title: Red Ring
content:
- Shroud's organization and technology line — the main enemy faction and the apparatus behind his engineered emergencies.
- Builds unstable energy cores (the Harbor Grid core; the device Nix cracks open displaying HELLO, CRUCIBLE) and tailored field tests used to study and pressure SDN's people.
- Recurs through probes, traps, stolen tech, convoy hits, and surveillance — every operation should feel connected to Shroud's larger plan.
- Give the Red Ring named hands and the sense of a single patient mind directing it, not just faceless operatives.
